page: Microsoft Azure E96as_v7 is an x86_64 architecture server belonging to the Easv7 family. It is powered by a dedicated AMD EPYC 9V45 processor running at 4.5 GHz, featuring 48 physical cores and 96 vCPUs via hyperthreading. The system is equipped with 768.0 GB of RAM, providing a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per vCPU. It does not include local storage or integrated GPUs, but it is Premium Storage capable.

Benchmark results place the E96as_v7 in the top tier for both single-core and multi-core CPU performance, as measured by stress-ng and Passmark CPU tests. Memory performance is also highly rated, with small-block cached read bandwidth reaching 9484.93 GB/sec and large-block write bandwidth at 174.51 GB/sec, both placing in the top 10% of tested systems. Database operations and Redis SET operations also perform in the top tier, with Redis achieving 139.15 million operations per second.

While the server delivers high performance for memory-intensive workloads, large-model LLM inference shows a performance tradeoff. Prompt processing for a 70B parameter model falls into the average category at 25.62 tokens/sec, though text generation remains strong at 6.45 tokens/sec. Given its high memory density and strong database benchmarks, this server is highly suited for large-scale relational databases, in-memory caching systems like Redis, data analytics, and memory-intensive enterprise applications.
description: The Microsoft Azure E96as_v7 is a memory-optimized virtual machine from the Easv7 family, powered by a dedicated AMD EPYC 9V45 processor. Operating at 4.5 GHz, it features 96 vCPUs across 48 physical cores and 768.0 GB of RAM, yielding an 8.0 GB per vCPU memory ratio. The server lacks local storage and GPUs but supports Premium Storage. In benchmark testing, the E96as_v7 demonstrates top-tier performance, ranking in the top 10% for single-core and multi-core CPU execution, small-block memory bandwidth, and Redis SET operations. However, a performance tradeoff is observed in large-model LLM prompt processing, which falls into the average tier. This hardware profile is highly suited for memory-intensive workloads, including large-scale database operations, in-memory caching, and enterprise data processing.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E96as_v7 is a memory-optimized server featuring 96 vCPUs on an AMD EPYC 9V45 processor, 768 GB of RAM, and top-tier CPU and memory bandwidth performance for database workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure E96as_v7 is a memory-optimized server with 96 vCPUs, 768 GB RAM, and a 4.5 GHz AMD EPYC processor, built for database workloads.
tagline: A memory-optimized virtual server featuring ninety-six vCPUs and high-bandwidth memory for demanding database and enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 96 vCPUs and 48 physical cores powered by a 4.5 GHz AMD EPYC 9V45 processor
768.0 GB of RAM providing a high 8.0 GB per vCPU memory allocation
Top-tier memory bandwidth and database performance in benchmark testing
No local storage or GPU, but compatible with Premium Storage options
Best suited for large-scale databases, in-memory caching, and data analytics
